title: carpinus turczaninovii var. coreana
intro: Carpinus turczaninovii var. coreana, also known as Korean hornbeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive horn-shaped branches and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Korean hornbeam include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve circulation, and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, infusions, and capsules, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
